subject: [PATCH v3 09/11] mm: handle PMD swap entry faults on swap-in

2026-07-03.patch_summary: This patch adds a new handler for PMD swap entry faults on swap-in, allowing the kernel to handle large page mappings in one shot. It introduces a new function do_huge_pmd_swap_page() that resolves the entire 2 MB mapping by looking up the folio in the swap cache, allocating a PMD-order folio if necessary, and restoring soft_dirty and uffd_wp from the swap PMD. The handler also updates vmf->orig_pmd and calls wp_huge_pmd() to COW immediately when the resulting PMD is read-only but the fault was a write. If PMD-order resources are unavailable, it splits the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries via __split_huge_pmd().

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Kairui Song noted that the patch does not handle the case where a PMD swap entry is split into PTEs, and requested that the handling of this case be added.

Reviewer Kairui Song noted that the patch does a synchronous swap-in without readahead, which makes sense due to the PMD size, but suggested mentioning this in the commit message for clarity and proposed using the return value of swapin_sync directly instead of handling its possible values separately.

Reviewer Kairui Song noted that repeated faults may occur when trying to lock a PMD-sized folio on a slower device, potentially leading to performance issues, and asked if this problem will worsen for PMDs.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Usama

Amazing work.

---

Hmm, so here we always do a sync swapin which means there is no
readahead, kind of make sense since PMD is huge, readahead might be
not helpful and a order 0 readahead breaks following PMD. But maybe
worth mention it in the commit message at least so it's easiler to
follow. (partly because the name swapin_sync is a bit confusing at the
moment, we could change that name as we improve swap readahead later).

And just an idea that will it be suffient if we just use the return
value of swapin_sync directly? If a smaller folio covers the PMD start
entry, the smaller folio is returned and you catched that just fine.
If a smaller folio covers a follow-up sub-entry or a sub-entry is
freed, -EBUSY is returned here and you just do the split. And it
doesn't do the allocation for the first lookup check, so there should
be no thrashing issue.

---

With PMD sized folio on a slower device, this try lock could fail much
more frequently, leading to repeated faults. That's not a new issue
and I remember Barry talked about it at the LSFMM this year, just I'm
not sure if that will get much worse for PMD?

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch adds a new handler for PMD swap entry faults during the swap-in process. The handler, do_huge_pmd_swap_page(), resolves the entire 2 MB mapping in one shot by looking up the folio in the swap cache and allocating a PMD-order folio if necessary. It then re-validates the folio and restores soft_dirty and uffd_wp from the swap PMD. If the resulting PMD is read-only but the fault was a write, it updates vmf->orig_pmd and calls wp_huge_pmd() to COW immediately. The patch also handles cases where PMD-order resources are unavailable by splitting the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries via __split_huge_pmd().

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2

Author acknowledged a concern about readahead not being useful for PMDs and agreed to add it to the commit message.

The author addressed a concern about zswap handling in PMD swap entry faults, explaining that zswap stores ranges as order-0 objects which can lead to non-updated large folios in the cache and subsequent SIGBUS errors.

The author acknowledged a potential issue with PMD-sized IO keeping the folio locked longer on slow swap devices, but agreed that the current behavior matches do_swap_page() and is not a tight retry loop. They suggested any improvement belongs to broader swap readahead/async swapin work.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kairui,

Thanks! and Thanks for the review comments!

---

Yes, I\u2019ll add this to the commit message. Thanks!

Yes readahead won't be useful for PMD as you said.

---

The main issue over here is zswap.
zswap still stores the range as order-0 objects. If we call PMD-order
swapin_sync() when the cache is empty but any slot is in zswap, zswap_load()
fails the large-folio load and leaves a non-uptodate PMD-sized folio in
swapcache. Splitting the PMD after that is not enough unless we also delete that
failed large folio, because the subsequent PTE fault can find the non-uptodate
large folio and SIGBUS instead of loading the individual zswap entries.

---

Agreed that PMD-sized IO can keep the folio locked longer, especially on
slow swap devices. (Which hopefully are dying away :))
The current behavior matches do_swap_page(): on the normal retryable fault
path it drops the fault lock and waits for the folio unlock before returning
VM_FAULT_RETRY, so it should not be a tight retry loop.
The wait can be longer for 2MB IO, but the retry usually comes back to
either an uptodate folio or a PMD that another fault already  installed.
I think this is the right baseline behavior, and any improvement here
belongs with the broader swap readahead / async swapin work.

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch adds a new handler for PMD swap entry faults during the swap-in process. The handler, do_huge_pmd_swap_page(), resolves the entire 2 MB mapping in one shot by looking up the folio in the swap cache and allocating a PMD-order folio if necessary. It then re-validates the folio and restores soft_dirty and uffd_wp from the swap PMD. If the resulting PMD is read-only but the fault was a write, it updates vmf->orig_pmd and calls wp_huge_pmd() to COW immediately. The patch also handles cases where PMD-order resources are unavailable by splitting the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries via __split_huge_pmd().
